what_it_does: This file is a curated reference list (a 'Sources' card) that collects external URLs about drag-and-drop, Apple Pencil squeeze/haptic gestures, long-press, radial-menu touchscreen usability, and iOS text-selection frustration.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-281a0920, type block, source claude_code, state current, title 'P10 · Sources', channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-a5b7cf79.
Body is a markdown bullet list of external sources with short labels and URLs.
Sources span Apple HIG, WWDC24 video, UX Stack Exchange threads, design blogs, Reddit/MacRumors user complaints, and NN/g article.
standouts: Explicitly references Apple Pencil 'squeeze-to-modify' plus haptic feedback from WWDC24.
Includes radial-menu-specific guidance about hand-occlusion, item limits, and icon-only design.
Mixes authoritative guidelines (Apple HIG, NN/g) with user-experience complaint threads (Reddit, MacRumors).
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, source, state, timestamps, and links.
Uses a markdown unordered list for references.
Labels each source with a concise, human-readable descriptor before the URL.
concerns: External URLs may rot; no archive or access dates are recorded.
No summary of how each source is intended to influence design.
No internal context explaining what 'P10' denotes.
notes: A reference-capture card collecting external UX/HIG sources on gestures, radial menus, and text selection for the dragnet project.
questions: How is each source expected to map to a specific design decision?
What is the parent item-a5b7cf79 and how does this source list relate to it?
What does the 'P10' prefix signify in the project plan?
Were these sources vetted or are they a raw reading list?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose ('Sources') matches the content exactly: it is a list of reference sources.
apparent_themes: Minimizing hand occlusion in touch UIs
Haptic and visual feedback for direct manipulation
Radial menu constraints and icon clarity
Evidence-based design using both guidelines and user reports
intention_signals: It appears to be gathering authoritative and community evidence to inform a touch/pen-based interaction system—likely involving drag-and-drop, radial menus, and text selection—for the dragnet project.
contribution: A labeled, topic-clustered set of external references that connects platform guidance (Apple HIG, WWDC24), established UX principles (NN/g, design blogs), and observed user pain points (forums) for gesture and selection design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item titled 'P10 · Sources' containing a curated bullet list of external URLs about drag-and-drop, Apple Pencil squeeze/haptic gestures, radial-menu touchscreen usability, and iOS text-selection frustration, with YAML metadata linking it to dragnet-development.
intention_for_embedding: To collect and organize reference material to inform design decisions for touch, pencil, and selection interactions.
